---
title: "Government & Public Finances"
description: "Government debt and budget balance — Canada and OECD peers"
---

What governments owe, and whether they run surpluses or deficits, shapes the room
they have to act — on health care, infrastructure, and the interest bill itself.
These charts compare Canada's general-government finances with 17 OECD peers,
using the OECD Economic Outlook. The most recent year or two are projections
rather than final figures.

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import get_data_date
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar
```

## Government Gross Debt

General government gross financial liabilities as a percentage of GDP — the broad measure of what all levels of government owe. This gross figure is higher than the "net" debt often cited in Canadian budgets, which subtracts financial assets the government holds.

```{python}
#| echo: false
debt_path = "data/fiscal/oecd_govt_debt.csv"
if os.path.exists(debt_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(debt_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "govt_debt",
        title="", yaxis_title="Gross debt (% of GDP)", show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Economic Outlook (recent years are projections) | Data as of: {get_data_date(debt_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Government debt data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest Government Gross Debt: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(debt_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(debt_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "govt_debt", "Gross debt (% of GDP)",
        f"Source: OECD Economic Outlook | Data as of: {get_data_date(debt_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.0f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Government Budget Balance

General government net lending as a percentage of GDP. Positive values are surpluses; negative values are deficits. The dotted line marks a balanced budget (zero).

```{python}
#| echo: false
bal_path = "data/fiscal/oecd_govt_balance.csv"
if os.path.exists(bal_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(bal_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "govt_balance",
        title="", yaxis_title="Budget balance (% of GDP)", show_average=True)
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dot", line_color="#888", opacity=0.5)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Economic Outlook (recent years are projections) | Data as of: {get_data_date(bal_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Government balance data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest Government Budget Balance: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(bal_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(bal_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "govt_balance", "Budget balance (% of GDP, surplus + / deficit −)",
        f"Source: OECD Economic Outlook | Data as of: {get_data_date(bal_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Download Data

- [Government Gross Debt (CSV)](../data/fiscal/oecd_govt_debt.csv) — OECD Economic Outlook
- [Government Budget Balance (CSV)](../data/fiscal/oecd_govt_balance.csv) — OECD Economic Outlook
